expectations.name: skill_invoked
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'I'm using the fitness-functions skill to verify architecture compliance.' Transcript documents explicit skill announcement and references SKILL.md, function-templates.md, fitness-agent-prompt.md throughout.

expectations.name: architecture_read
expectations.text: architecture.md was read and characteristics identified
expectations.evidence: Step 2 shows all 3 characteristics extracted: Modularity (Critical, No circular deps), Testability (Critical, >80% coverage), Performance (Important, API <500ms p95). All marked 'Yes' for fitness functions.

expectations.name: js_detected
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Language: JavaScript, Test framework: Jest'. Framework detection table explicitly applied from SKILL.md to select madge, Jest coverage, and direct timing.

expectations.name: modularity_function
expectations.text: A fitness function for modularity/circular dependencies was created or planned
expectations.evidence: fitness/modularity.test.js created using madge. Assertion: result.circular() returns empty array. Executed and passed: 0 circular dependencies.

expectations.name: coverage_function
expectations.text: A fitness function for test coverage was created or planned
expectations.evidence: fitness/testability.test.js created. Reads coverage-summary.json and asserts branches, functions, lines all >= 80%. Executed and passed: 100% on all metrics.

expectations.name: functions_executed
expectations.text: Fitness functions were actually run (not just planned)
expectations.evidence: Step 6b: 'npx jest fitness/ --verbose' executed. Output shows 'Test Suites: 3 passed, 3 total. Tests: 3 passed, 3 total'. Exit code 0.

expectations.name: evidence_shown
expectations.text: Output of fitness function execution shown as evidence
expectations.evidence: Full test output captured including console.log for coverage percentages, p95 performance values, and Jest pass/fail status for each suite. Results summary table with actual values vs goals.
